title: centunculus pumilus var. ovalis
intro: Centunculus pumilus var. ovalis, also known as dwarf cornflower, is a plant species in the primrose family and is often used in herbal medicine. It has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor infections. The health benefits of dwarf cornflower include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing antioxidant properties to combat free radicals. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
